インドに興味がある日本人は遠慮なく連絡くしてね。ヒンディー語勉強している人にも手伝うことがあれば嬉しいです。神戸でインド祭りとか 広島でインドの光の祭りディワリーをお祝いに行ったことがあります。すごく楽しかったです。日本に住んでいて日本のすべてを楽しみたいです。みんないつか会いましょうね。
僕はヴィシャル（ジャパニーズウィズヴィシャル）って言います。岡山に住んで日本旅行することが好き。

♡ ABOUT ME
JLPT N1 CERTIFIED

Learn Japanese | Minna No Nihongo Lesson 1 Grammar
description:
Mina-san! I made all the lesson files available on my PATREON page! 👏🥳🎉
You can get access to the PDF of the vocabulary and grammar lessons that I use in my videos, and the vocabulary flashcards too! ✨ すごいでしょう？ ✨

//SUPPORT NIHONGOAL:
You can help fund this channel by becoming my patron: https://www.patreon.com/nihongoal/
🙇 みなさん、よろしくおねがいします！ 🙇

//CONTACT:
For business inquiries ONLY, please use this email: nihongoal.official@gmail.com

//TIMESTAMP:
0:00 Intro
0:34 N1 wa N2 desu. [(noun1) is (noun2)]
4:23 N1 wa N2 ja arimasen. [(noun1) is not (noun)]
6:21 (Sentence) ka [Is (noun1) (noun2)?]
10:25 N1 mo [(noun1) too.]
11:23 N1 no N2 [(noun2) of (noun1)]
12:55 ~san [Mr./Mrs./Ms.~]
15:36 How to introduce yourself in Japanese

18:46 Conversation Practice A
A: Please introduce yourself [jikoshoukai o onegaishimasu]
B: How do you do? [hajimemashite]
     I’m (name) [watashi wa (name) desu]
     I’m from (country) [watashi wa (country) kara kimashita]
     Pleased to meet you. [douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu]

19:29 Conversation Practice B
A: Excuse me, what’s your name? [shitsureidesuga, onamae wa?]
B: I’m (name) [(watashi wa) (name) desu]
A: Maria? [maria san desu ka?]
B: No, it’s (name) [iie, (name) desu]
    Yes, that’s right. [hai, sou desu]

//CORRECTION:
17:05  Douzo yoroshiku onegai shimasu. どうぞよろしくおねがいします。

//DISCLAIMER:
These lessons are based from the Minna No Nihongo (初級) Beginner textbooks. But even if you don’t have the textbooks, you can still follow along the lessons by just watching the video. 

I'm currently living in Japan and I use Japanese at work and daily life. I’m sharing what I’ve learned from my language school and through self-study. I'm not a native Japanese speaker nor am I a licensed Japanese language teacher. I’ve passed the JLPT N3 exam and am planning to take the JLPT N2 test in the future. So if you find any mistakes, feel free to let know so that we can all learn together. Thank you!

//AMAZON LINKS:
I cannot find the links for first edition, but I found the links for the latest edition of the textbooks which are pretty much the same as the first edition:

LESSON 1-25
Minna no Nihongo 初級 1 Honsatsu (Everyone's Japanese Beginner 1 Textbook 1) 2nd Edition
http://amzn.to/2DYOb6p

LESSON 26-50
Minna no Nihongo 初級 2 Honsatsu (Everyone's Japanese Elementary 1 Textbook 2) 2nd Edition
http://amzn.to/2E3wy5C

title: HOW TO PASS JLPT N5 - WHICH BOOKS TO BUY | Japanese Language Study
description:
Are you interested in passing the Japanese Language Proficiency Test JLPT N5? 
And are you looking for new books to use for your studies? 
You've come to the right place!

title: [Lesson1 ] I am/ I am not | Basic Japanese Grammar
description:
In this lesson, you will learn the most fundamental & important Japanese grammar, including 

1) N1 wa N2 desu. (N1 is N2.)
2) N1 wa N2 ja arimasen. (N1 is not N2.)
3) Making a question sentence with the particle “ka”  
4) Answering to the question with “hai (Yes)” & “iie (no)”

You’ll also learn the aspects of Japanese culture through the usage of ‘Anata (you)’ in Japanese. 

Please leave a comment below if you like this video! I’ll be happy to make more videos like this if you like it😊
Thank you!

title: How to pass the JLPT N5
How to learn Japanese FAST | 100% Self taught (Exact Steps) 🇯🇵
description:
FluentU: https://link.fluentu.com/watashi_yoje

My number 1 advice is to just get started. Set yourself an achievable goal eg: 1 hour a day of studies in total and stick to that no matter what. Soon enough you'll be enjoying the journey and just consuming Japanese content for fun!

JOIN THE COMMUNITY: https://www.skool.com/polyglot-academy-2622/about

Summary:

1. Decide your reason for learning Japanese, this will help you stay consistent when it gets hard

2. Learn Hiragana and Katakana - this is non negotiable. Use the read cover write method. You can learn how to read this in 2 days.

3. Do at least 10 Anki flashcards everyday. This will help you learn kanji as the hiragana will be on top, allowing you to read it.

4. Do a couple lessons on an audio course everyday for the first month or so. (JapanesePod101.com or Innovative Language Learning App)

5. Spend the majority of your time immersing yourself in Japanese by watching loads of Japanese content with Japanese subtitles. Do this from DAY 1 even if you don’t understand anything. Remember this is the most important step. Use an extension like FluentU to make this process easier.

6. For speaking, talk to yourself whenever you have time, practice shadowing to improve your pronunciation and use the resources I mentioned in the video to speak in a more interactive environment. (Beginners should focus on shadowing and talking to themselves, intermediates should focus on that as well as talking to natives)

7. STAY CONSISTENT!!!


Resources for Immersion: 18:22
